name: Barbara Tropp's China Moon Cafe Wonton Burgers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup cilantro or Chinese chives, chopped
1 1/2 tablespoons Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
3 pounds pork butt (ground with a fat-to-lean ratio of 1:3 to 1:4)
2 French baguettes, each cut crosswise into 4 equal portions
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the green onions, cilantro, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, Chinese rice wine or dry sherry, salt, pepper, hot chili oil, and chicken stock. Mix well.
2. Add the pork and stir gently with your hands or a large spoon until the mixture can be thoroughly blended without overcooking the meat. Optionally, refrigerate overnight to allow flavors to develop; bring to room temperature before cooking.
3. Handling the meat mixture as little as possible, divide it into 8 equal portions. Form each portion into rectangles to fit the bread.
4. Brush a heavy skillet with a thin layer of peanut oil.
5. Place the pan over high heat and cook until very hot. Add the patties and sear, turning once, until well browned on both sides. Reduce the heat as needed and continue cooking until the patties are done to your preference.
6. Cut each French baguette lengthwise and spread mustard on the cut surfaces. Enclose the patties inside the baguettes.
7. Serves 8.
NOTE: "Burgers made from leftover spicy pork wonton filling is a favorite staff lunch at China Moon Cafe in San Francisco. The meat mixture is moist and zestily seasoned, so a mild Dijon mustard is an excellent accompaniment. Wonton translates as 'cosmic chaos,' referring to the universe described as a jumble of light and dark enclosed in a thin shell."
